page: Microsoft Azure D64s_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DSv4 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 32 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8272CL x86_64 processor operating at 2.6 GHz. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, relying instead on external Premium Storage capabilities for persistent disk operations.

Benchmark evaluations position the D64s_v4 primarily in the average tier for general compute tasks, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1322.0 and a multi-core score of 12579.0. However, the architecture demonstrates strong capabilities in memory bandwidth, achieving 212.55 GB/sec for large-block read-only operations and 95.66 GB/sec for large-block write operations, both placing in the top 25% of tested systems. Extended CPU instructions also perform strongly, as evidenced by a Passmark CPU extended instructions score of 54545.81, aided by hardware support for AVX-512 vector extensions.

The lack of local storage and dedicated GPU hardware represents a clear tradeoff, making this instance unsuitable for workloads requiring high-speed local scratch disks or hardware-accelerated graphics processing. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the D64s_v4 offers a balanced resource density of compute and memory, making it a stable option for enterprise workloads that scale horizontally. It is suited for multi-threaded applications, database operations, static web serving, and software compilation tasks that benefit from its 64 dedicated vCPUs and strong memory bandwidth.
description: Microsoft Azure D64s_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DSv4 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL x86_64 processor running on Microsoft Hyper-V. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs (32 physical cores) and 256.0 GB of RAM, maintaining a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-memory ratio. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, requiring external Premium Storage for disk operations. Performance benchmarks indicate average single-core and multi-core compute capabilities, alongside strong memory bandwidth performance, with large-block reads reaching 212.55 GB/sec. This configuration is suited for multi-threaded enterprise workloads, database operations, software compilation, and static web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D64s_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 64 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and strong memory bandwidth, suited for multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D64s_v4 is a general-purpose VM with 64 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and strong memory bandwidth, but no local storage.
tagline: A balanced x86_64 virtual machine featuring sixty-four dedicated vCPUs and strong memory bandwidth for multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with 64 dedicated vCPUs and 32 physical cores
256.0 GB of system memory maintaining a balanced 1:4 resource ratio
Strong memory bandwidth performance for large-block read and write operations
No local storage, requiring external Premium Storage for persistent disks
Suited for multi-threaded enterprise applications, databases, and web serving
